title: [FIRRTL][ExportVerilog] Don't Generate Unnecessary Wires for Unused Output Ports
body:
For situations where the output of a module is truly unused, we should either:
1. Remove this port from the module
2. Don't emit a dead wire for this port

Consider the following FIRRTL input. Output `c` of module `Sub` is unused:

```scala
circuit UnusedOutput:
  module Sub:
    input a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>
    output c: UInt<1>

    b <= a
    c <= not(a)

  module UnusedOutput:
    input a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>

    inst sub of Sub
    sub.a <= a
    b <= sub.b
```

Currently, CIRCT will compile this to (`firtool UnusedOutput.fir -strip-debug-info`):

```verilog
module Sub(
  input  a,
  output b,
         c);

  assign b = a;
  assign c = ~a;
endmodule

module UnusedOutput(
  input  a,
  output b);

  wire _sub_c;

  Sub sub (
    .a (a),
    .b (b),
    .c (_sub_c)
  );
endmodule
```

This includes the dead wire `_sub_c`.  It would be better to either remove the port, option (1):

```verilog
module Sub(
  input  a,
  output b);

  assign b = a;
  assign c = ~a;
endmodule

module UnusedOutput(
  input  a,
  output b);

  Sub sub (
    .a (a),
    .b (b),
  );
endmodule
```

Or to just not connect the port of `sub` to anything, option (2):
```verilog
module Sub(
  input  a,
  output b,
         c);

  assign b = a;
  assign c = ~a;
endmodule

module UnusedOutput(
  input  a,
  output b);

  Sub sub (
    .a (a),
    .b (b),
    .c (/* unused */)
  );
endmodule
```

The motivation for this change is to avoid lint warnings about unused wires.
